name: Mirror Light Reflection
description: Mirror Light Reflection is a rule-based challenge that asks the player to observe, reason, manipulate state, or sequence actions.
design_notes: Mirror Light Reflection should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For puzzle mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use mirror light reflection as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect mirror light reflection to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels clever when they infer the rule and manipulate the system correctly.

parameters.name: activation_condition
parameters.description: Rule that determines when the puzzle element changes state.

parameters.name: reset_time
parameters.description: Delay before the puzzle returns to its default state.

parameters.name: feedback_intensity
parameters.description: How strongly the game communicates success, failure, or partial progress.

edge_cases: Mirror Light Reflection is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Mirror Light Reflection overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Mirror Light Reflection runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Mirror Light Reflection state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Mirror Light Reflection plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Mirror Light Reflection produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Mirror Light Reflection strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Mirror Light Reflection becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Mirror Light Reflection with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Mirror Light Reflection as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Mirror Light Reflection with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Mirror Light Reflection as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
